reviews.author: Sven Niederloehner
reviews.text: Yes it is expensive and a little bit complicated to get there. But thats maybe one of the best beaches i have ever been to. Looks just stunning. They also have people there cleaning its very organized there. Loved every second there.

reviews.author: Le
reviews.text: Ky Co Beach is a beautiful beach that some have called the "Maldives of Vietnam". We visited during the off season, but during peak season, there’s water activities such as swimming, snorkeling, and kayaking. Bring sunscreen, sunglasses, bug spray and a waterproof bag. Walking along the beach with the soft sand below our feet and the back drop of the beautiful tropical scenery. We stopped by this unique "stairway to heaven" for some amazing photo opportunities. The restaurant was under renovation, but I bet they would serve up the best, fresh seafood and exotic drinks. Also make sure to make the short drive over to nearby to Eo Gió (Wind Strait) to enjoy the walk way that hugs the cliffs.

reviews.author: Danielle
reviews.text: Don't waste your money. Total rip off. 170 to get on to the beach and 100 dong for transport. Beach is run down and dirty. Not sure what the fee is for when they can't even keep it clean. Also, no swimming allowed as out of season.

reviews.author: Omar Osman
reviews.text: The beach is great but a few points to note. You can not swim except in summer time. I came in January and it was forbidden to swim due to high waves. The ticket is so expensive for a beach entry. 120k dongs+ mandatory bus 110k dongs, so 330k dongs.

reviews.author: Paul Parent
reviews.text: Kỳ Co Beach, located near Nhơn Lý in Quy Nhơn, is often called the “Maldives of Vietnam” for its crystal-clear turquoise waters and powdery white sand. Nestled between rugged mountains and the sea, the beach feels like a hidden paradise, offering both dramatic scenery and tranquil beauty. For photographers, Kỳ Co is a dream destination. The contrast between the emerald sea, golden sand, and rocky cliffs creates striking compositions. Aerial shots with a drone capture the full curve of the shoreline, while sunrise and sunset provide soft, golden light that enhances the natural colors. The shallow waters near the shore are incredibly clear, perfect for capturing reflections and underwater scenes. Beyond its beauty, Kỳ Co offers opportunities for snorkeling, swimming, and exploring nearby coves, adding variety to both travel experiences and photo stories. Local seafood stalls near the beach provide a cultural touch, where visitors can enjoy freshly prepared dishes against the backdrop of the ocean. Kỳ Co Beach is more than just a seaside escape—it is a destination where unspoiled nature meets vibrant coastal life. For travelers and photographers, it remains one of the most captivating beaches in central Vietnam.

reviews.author: Alexandrina Todorova
reviews.text: Not worth it or maybe wrong season but felt like a tourist trap. We went at the end of December with the idea to swim, once we arrived at the Welcome gate, we were told no swimming allowed. The Grab driver was waiting for us to go to the Ward Gate, where the shuttle is taking you to the beach. All the facilities were closed and/or broken, waves were huge, so obviously no swimming. Workers were the only people on the beach laying around under the palm trees. Yes, it's a beautiful beach destroyed by the greed and nothing even close to the "Maldives of Asia". Very disappointing, don't waste your time and money for this place.

reviews.author: An Du Thinh
reviews.text: Beach itself is lovely with clear water, clean smooth sand, but the experience of being charged for entrance/parking/transport to just get to the beach which should be a publicly accessible place leaves a sour taste unfortunately. Sun-beds with shade are also pricey…

reviews.author: Hong Anh Pham
reviews.text: Clean beach with clear water. Some services are provided like place to sit, water sports and seal circuits for children but not the top quality.

reviews.author: chui
reviews.text: They were charging 170k for the entry fee plus another 150k for motorbike transport. In the off-season, this is definitely not worth it. After hearing the price, I decided not to go and went to Eo Gio instead—which turned out to be a great choice. The entry fee there was only 22k, by the way.

reviews.author: Rokas Didrikas
reviews.text: Even though it's a touristy place, but the views are amazing. We went there around 8am and it wasn't crowded.

reviews.author: 雨姮
reviews.text: Ky Co beach is clean, the wave is quite strong. I went to Ky Co beach twice, by both motorboat and motorbike, each kind of transportation had its own pros and cons. Going by motorboat was faster, and it could bring tourists to see coral reefs or to Hon Kho beach. But it made me feel seasick because of the strong wave. And for riding a motorbike, I had to leave Ky Co before 5 PM to catch the last shuttle bus, and I could see Nhon Ly village from above.

reviews.author: Nguyen Henry
reviews.text: The beach is crystal clear but a bit too crownded in the weekend. The way to get here is a bit challenges but they provide car services when you purchase the ticket.

reviews.author: Reene Helberg
reviews.text: We visited in the beginning of November. We drove by car to the gate. Paid the entrance fee 170 k / per adult, kids did not have to pay (ours were 5 and 6 yo). Then our driver entered and took us to where the shuttles take the tourists to the beach. We were there about 9 or 10 in the morning and there was very little people. Everyone but us were Vietnamese tourists. The shuttle took us to the beach and once we reached there the sealion show was just about to begin. It was a nice show and it is included in the ticket price. The beach is beautiful! Unfortunately waves were so big it was impossible to swim so we just walked around and enjoyed the view.

reviews.author: Hảo
reviews.text: Quy Nhon and Ky Co Beach stole a piece of my heart. Don't get me wrong, the journey to get there wasn't exactly luxurious. It involved a bit of a bus ride and then a bumpy car trip, but trust me, it's all worth it when you see that beach. The sand is like powdered sugar, soft and white between your toes, and the water? Crystal clear turquoise that stretches out forever. You can see right down to the bottom, it's incredible. There's something special about Ky Co. It feels untouched, wild even. Maybe it's because it's a bit off the beaten path, but there weren't tons of tourists around. I spent most of the day just swimming, sunbathing, and soaking up the scenery. There are some local vendors selling snacks and drinks, but other than that, it's pure relaxation. Now, a heads up for anyone who's not a strong swimmer: the water gets deep pretty quickly. It wasn't a problem for me, but something to keep in mind, especially if you're traveling with little ones. Overall, Ky Co was pure paradise. If you're looking for a beach escape that feels authentic and unspoiled, this is your place.

reviews.author: Viet Nguyen Van
reviews.text: The most clean and beautiful beach i have seen in my life. Price is so low for 1 boat trip from the port to the beach, it's like 10 dollars each person. Food around the town is fresh and reasonable price. One of the best place to take real pictures for your self when walking around the town and discovering the real life of local people out there. I have to come back over 3 times to enjoy the food here and take more chances to spend money here. I feel like the more i spend the more i save if comparing to big cities around like Sai Gon or Ha Noi. I'm thinking to buy a single house or an apartment right there, price is about 50.000 dollars to retire in next 10-15 years later.

reviews.author: Dominik Lindner
reviews.text: I really liked the place. The beach is clean, quite spacious and even when there are many visitors it doesn't feel crowded. The water quality was very good (incredible colour) and nice waves. There are plenty of places to hide in the shade and clean toilets and showers. As this beach attracts many Asian tourists, there are plenty of opportunities to take some cool pictures. Prices in the restaurants were reasonable and the quality was good. We arrived by boat as our visit was part of a package tour. Although we visited on a Saturday, the beach was not crowded. Would come back again

reviews.author: cheng u ong
reviews.text: the beach is clean and beautiful. The water is crystal clear and cold! There are a few seats that u can sit for foc (find urself) The entrance fee is 120k +50k bus ticket. If u are riding motor,u need an extra paid 10k The ticket is quite expensive. Recommend going to another beach which is foc

reviews.author: Peter Perháč, MBA
reviews.text: Beautiful, clean beach. When I went, the waves were strong so I couldn't swim. The seal show was nice. But entrance fee 170000 dg is so high. Therefore 5* for the beach and 3* for the entrance fee

reviews.author: tung tran.thanh
reviews.text: The beach is beautiful. It is rather hard to go there. 25km from Qui Nhon city. There also the way go by cano. Having a bath is free. We can try the diving service, flying with the hang gliding. Alot of check in point to take photo.

reviews.author: Magherlyn Casique
reviews.text: Exactly as stated in the other reviews. 170k entry fee, then you have to use their bus (scooters not allowed) and then you cannot even swim. Huge tourist trap and not recommended!

reviews.author: Kelvin Pham
reviews.text: Wonderful beach I ever ever had in my life. The beach so clean and clear. Can see through under sea water. Many sightseeings for us to take photos.

reviews.author: yến nhi nguyễn
reviews.text: This is like a private beach that you have to pay ticket to enter (about 8$) by road, or book a private boat tour includes seafood meal and sightseeing some other points in 4-6 hours (about 25$/person). In my opinion, I prefer the boat trip which is carried on by the locals village men, they will find you and ask, sure that you don't have to find them. The beach is clean, clear water but wavy most of the time, and will be crowed on weedkend. I like this beach but I don't like to pay to much for each time coming here, I stayed in Quy Nhon for a month, wanted to visit a nice beach multiple times, this beach is not my choice.

reviews.author: Tadd Pham
reviews.text: Beautiful little beach with calm and shallow water. There's no direct beach access. Either using speedboat access from the South or drive/taxi to "bãi đậu xe Nhơn Lý 1" or "cổng vào Kỳ Co" and pay 170 ngàn per person. They will provide transportation to the beach. Once at the Kỳ Co beach, there is service to "Hòn Sẹo" for 200 ngàn per person by speedboat (transportation and snorkel only); the diving option will cost more.

reviews.author: Hang Luu
reviews.text: Ky Co is a fantastic spot to visit in Quy Nhon, compared to Eo Gio and Hon Kho. All these scenic points are quite overcrowded and packed with tourists. However, I recommend you visit Ky Co in the early morning or in the week days to avoid the crowd. The water in Ky Co is crystal transparent and she can cool you down in summer heat! They also offer free shows with funny and cute seals at different time during the day. Very attractive to kids.

reviews.author: idgaf
reviews.text: The sea here is one of the clerest water I've ever swim in. Fish swimming everywhere, the jet ski and the parachute gliding is insanely satisfying. If you come to Quy Nhơn, you definitely have to come here.

reviews.author: Jacq Mouton
reviews.text: You never know what you're gonna get in Vietnam but this place is a pleasant surprise. One of the best beaches I've been to in Vietnam. Pristine with good facilities. The water is crystal clear turquoise. The sand is white and the waves are stunning. The staff take great care of making sure the beach is free from plastic. This is one of the least polluted beaches I've been to in Vietnam. It can only be reached via boat or the shuttle from inside the park. No nosy traders/ people that bother you. The beach doesn't smell of gas from boats like many other beaches. The Entrance fee at the gate is 120k VND and 40K for a shuttle to the beach (July 2023). From the gate we drove about 3km to the point where the shuttle transfers you to the beach. Along the way there is a beautiful view of the fishing village. The wind is quite strong at the top so be careful with your hat. The shuttle to and from the beach is comfortable. The facilities at the beach are plentiful. There are many beach chairs and canopies that you can rent @ 300k for the day. Beer goes for 25-30k, some finger snacks for 25k. The restaurant caters for tour groups, but there is a seafood restaurant in the resort right on the beach. The bathroom is really clean and they have many places to shower off sand. I would recommend coming here in the afternoon when the tour groups are less or during weekdays. The sun is hot so remember sunscreen. The wind picks up more in the afternoon so there can be some sandblasting. Here you can go for a walk along the cliff with beautiful views of the clear water down below. There are many cool spots for taking photographs. Swimming and playing in the waves here was the best part of the day. The ocean here is so stunning, just a little paradise. Note: Little kids might find this beach scary as the waves can get big and the wind can be strong.

reviews.author: Phil Langdon
reviews.text: Beautiful beach 5 minutes by boat from Tour KY Co Eo Gio. Many photo opportunities and beautiful clean water to swim in. Big restaurant has large menu and of course it a little expensive. Well trained seal performance every couple hours. And NO! The seals are not harmed or tortured in anyway. I asked one of the seals. Well worth a day trip.

reviews.author: Dominik Vu
reviews.text: For 170k / person, we weren't sure about it but it turned our great. Off season - nobody there, which was great! However, this comes with limited services, no swimming, and less food options. I actually enjoyed being the only group there and I wasn't ready to swim anyway. Be mindful that winter is quite windy, so you probably will have limited swimming options. Place is quiet, you can see the remains of a typical vietnamese early 2000s resort - tasteless decorations and questionable use of space, however, that just adds to the vibe. It was nice to see a beach with no trash.

reviews.author: Thuy Vy Le
reviews.text: Amazing beach. We are happy to spend time here. Sand is quite white, and clean. There are a lot of decorations at beach for taking nice photos.

reviews.author: Uyển Nhi Hồ
reviews.text: ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Kỳ Co Beach is absolutely beautiful! 🏝️ Clear turquoise water, soft white sand, and stunning mountain views all around. The place is quite clean and well-maintained — definitely worth a visit when in Quy Nhơn! 🌊☀️✨
